title: Update Home hero image to building and remove 'Our Properties' label on About page

requirements.text: Update the Home page hero background image to use a new building-themed image (instead of the current "/assets/Screenshot 2026-02-16 030700.png").

quotes: change the home picture as buliding and remove the  name as our properties in about page

acceptanceCriteria: Home page hero uses a building-themed image asset path (not "/assets/Screenshot 2026-02-16 030700.png").
Hero section layout, animations, and CTA buttons remain unchanged aside from the image swap.

requirements.text: Add the newly generated building image to the frontend as a static asset under "frontend/public/assets/generated" and reference it from the Home page hero.

quotes: change the home picture as buliding

acceptanceCriteria: A new image file exists in "frontend/public/assets/generated" with the exact filename specified in imageRequirements.required.
The Home page references the new file via a public URL path (e.g., "/assets/generated/...png").

requirements.text: Remove the text label "Our Properties" from the About page featured image section while keeping the rest of the section styling/layout intact (icon, image, card container, spacing, and animations).

quotes: remove the  name as our properties in about page

acceptanceCriteria: The About page no longer displays the heading text "Our Properties".
The featured image section still renders correctly with consistent spacing and styling (no broken layout).

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui or any path listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Keep all changes within the existing React + Tailwind + TanStack Router structure (no new backend services).
nonGoals: Changing the About page main title "SIVAKUMAR" or page metadata.
Changing the About page featured image itself (only remove the "Our Properties" label).

required: Modern real-estate building exterior hero photo with clean architectural lines, daylight, wide composition (home-hero-building.dim_1920x1080.png)
